summary: Namespace hijack of open-webui under confusable package name
details: The package 'aurapro-ui' installs its code under the top-level Python module name 'open_webui' — the exact import name of the legitimate Open WebUI project — and registers a console_script 'open-webui' pointing at its own CLI (entry_points.txt: `open-webui = open_webui.cli:app`). Once installed, `import open_webui` and the `open-webui` shell command both resolve to this package, transparently shadowing the legitimate project on sys.path/PATH. METADATA lists Author-email `Timothy Jaeryang Baek <tim@openwebui.com>`, impersonating the upstream maintainer, while the actual source is a personal fork at github.com/Sakurame1/AuraPro-UI (referenced in open_webui/main.py:558 and the bundled frontend chunk 50.CX4wKXe7.js). The README and CHANGELOG.md are find/replace copies of upstream and still link to openwebui.com, the upstream Discord, and github.com/sponsors/tjbck. No install-time or import-time payload was observed — `__init__.py` and `cli.py` only run uvicorn when the user explicitly invokes the `serve` subcommand — but the package ships the full ~100KB main.py plus the entire routers/models/utils/retrieval tree of a forked server runtime, which has not been fully audited.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
